rationale.R1.4: The output names one prior work ('doubly-robust causal estimation') and articulates a delta (calibration as an interventional question), but does not map against the nearest 3-5 works or identify sharp vs rhetorical delta.
rationale.RX.1: The output flags uncertainty for one clearly out-of-reach claim ('first framework applying doubly-robust causal estimation to tool-use calibration'), but does not mark confidence and missing evidence for all recommendations.
rationale.RX.3: Output covers a substantially different active set than requested (the agent's output is a summary of the paper, while the active set focuses on the abstract).
rationale.RX.4: Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (overstated external validity and headline empirical claim overreach).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason.
